title: Transurethral Resection of the Prostate -- Technique or Treatment -- Pearls: Summary of Tips for Performing Large Prostates by TURP
content: Do not attempt a larger prostate than you can reasonably handle within 90 minutes of operating time. Have everything ready and prepared, so you don't have to stop for supplies, instruments, or catheters. Be prepared to stop after just completing one side if it's taking too long. It is always possible to return another day to finish if necessary. Establish a regular rhythm. Make full-length slices from the bladder neck to the veru. Do not waste time cauterizing bleeding sites unless vision is seriously impaired or the capsule is reached. The resection does not have to be perfect; just good enough. Try to leave some extra time at the end to search for bleeders. Using video can slow you down. Consider using just direct vision. The position of the surgeon's head will prevent inadvertent distal repositioning of the scope. It is a good idea to learn how to do the TURP both with video guidance and direct vision in case there is a problem with the video in the middle of the case.

title: Transurethral Resection of the Prostate -- Technique or Treatment -- Discussion
content: There are also some disadvantages. There are essentially three distinct, separate stages to the surgery, and it would be difficult to quickly stop the procedure until all the surgical steps are completed. The initial groove made at the roof is done without any visual landmarks in an area where the prostate is the thinnest. Any early bleeding or perforation will significantly increase the complication rate and severity. The lateral lobes can easily overcrowd the prostatic fossa, hide bleeding sites, and not leave enough room for easy resection due to interference with the mobility of the resectoscope. Any early bleeding can be difficult to see or control with bulky lateral lobe tissue in the way.

title: Transurethral Resection of the Prostate -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
content: TURP is typically an elective procedure used to treat prostatic enlargement resulting in symptomatic bladder outlet obstruction. Because of this, appropriate indications and discussion of the complications associated with the procedure require an integrated interprofessional team to communicate effectively and initiate treatment plans so the patient and family have appropriate expectations. This approach will ensure that patients are adequately prepared for their procedure and what to expect. Patients who are better informed are more invested in their decision and are happier with their outcomes. The procedure is elective and irreversible, so some extra review and discussion with the patient and family are reasonable.

title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 1 -- Community 225
content: Prostate cancer (سرطان البروستاتا), including the specific entity malignant tumor involving seminal vesicle by direct extension from prostate (Malignant tumor involving seminal vesicle by direct extension from prostate), is a malignant prostate tumor (أورام البروستاتا الخبيثة (SMQ)) that arises in the prostate (prostata) and may extend into the seminal vesicles (semenné váčky). It can progress to metastatic prostate cancer (سرطان البروستاتا النَّقيلي) and is clinically associated with a spectrum of genitourinary symptoms, prostate disorder (اضطراب بروستاتي), ureteral stone (حصاة حالبية), and benign prostatic hyperplasia (فرط تنسج بروستاتي حميد) or prostate enlargement (تضخم بروستاتي). The prostate serves as the anatomic site for these diseases and for surgical interventions such as open prostatectomy, which excises the prostate and may include removal of malignant tumors and partial involvement of the seminal vesicles, and transurethral resection of the prostate (TURP). TURP is performed primarily for benign prostatic hyperplasia and prostate enlargement, but it is also clinically associated with prostate cancer, being used for diagnostic evaluation or therapeutic management, and its indication may be influenced by the presence of cancer. Additional male genital disorders are considered subcategories of prostate disorder and are linked to screening activities (التحصّي). This network of relationships underscores the interconnected nature of prostate pathology, its potential spread to adjacent structures, and the role of surgical procedures in patient management.

title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 3 -- Community 1723
content: Transurethral resection of prostate (TURP) is clinically associated with فرط تنسج بروستاتي حميد (benign prostatic hyperplasia), تضخم بروستاتي (prostatic enlargement), and سرطان البروستاتا (prostate cancer). TURP is commonly performed to relieve urinary obstruction caused by benign prostatic hyperplasia and other forms of prostatic enlargement, providing symptomatic improvement and facilitating bladder emptying. In the context of prostate cancer, TURP may be used to obtain tissue for histopathological diagnosis or to palliate obstructive symptoms in advanced disease. By addressing both benign and malignant prostatic conditions, TURP plays a pivotal role in the therapeutic and diagnostic management of patients with prostate-related disorders.
